handoff_summary_for_next_iteration:
This round made no code changes; the crate still builds successfully and ends with only the same two private_interfaces warnings about Shc::write_c and Shc::make returning the private ShcError type.

Effective/ineffective: effectively, leaving behavior unchanged preserved the successful build and the round was accepted as the new baseline. Ineffective in the sense that no warning cleanup was performed, so the visibility mismatch remains.

What to avoid next round: avoid broad refactors or touching unrelated logic, since the project is already compiling; also avoid changing error/public API visibility casually without checking intended crate-level exposure.

What to prioritize next round: if doing cleanup, focus narrowly on resolving the ShcError visibility mismatch in src/shc.rs, either by reducing method visibility or making the error type appropriately visible, while preserving the current successful compile state.
